scenario_description: A user tracks a specific bright blue woven beach bag from a sunny beach setting to a dimly lit museum interior, then to a crowded street scene with similar bags, requiring cross-turn visual memory to identify the original item.

turns.text: Just got back from the coast. Here's my favorite bag on the sand.
turns.image_description: A close-up shot of a bright azure blue woven straw beach bag sitting upright on white sand. The bag has thick brown leather handles and a small red tassel hanging from the zipper pull. Next to it is a folded terracotta-colored towel. The lighting is harsh, direct sunlight creating sharp shadows.

turns.text: That's a gorgeous color! The contrast between the blue bag and the white sand looks great. Did you take it inside somewhere else later?
Yeah, I brought it into this museum gift shop area. It looked pretty different under the gallery lights.
turns.image_description: An indoor shot taken in a dimly lit museum hallway. The same bright azure blue woven bag is now slumped over the arm of a dark wooden bench near a glass display case. The artificial yellowish lighting makes the blue appear slightly darker and duller. The red tassel is visible but less vibrant due to the low light. No towel is present.

turns.text: It does look quite deflated there compared to the beach photo. The lighting really changes the vibe. Is that still the only one you have?
I found a market stall selling them. Look at this crowd of bags.
turns.image_description: A busy outdoor market stall displaying three woven beach bags hanging side-by-side. From left to right: A deep navy blue bag with gold tassels; a bright azure blue bag (identical weave and shape to the first image) with a red tassel; and a teal green bag with silver charms. The lighting is overcast daylight.

turns.text: Wow, they all look very similar in style. It's hard to tell which one matches your story just by looking at the colors in this light.

Which of these three bags in the market photo is the exact same one I showed you at the beginning? A) The navy blue one with gold tassels B) The bright azure one with the red tassel C) The teal green one with silver charms

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: 1. In Turn 1, the user introduces a 'bright azure blue' bag with a 'red tassel'. 2. In Turn 3, the user confirms this is the same bag, noting its appearance under gallery lights, maintaining the red tassel detail. 3. In Turn 5, three options are presented: Navy/Gold, Azure/Red, and Teal/Silver. 4. Only option B matches the specific combination of color (azure) and accessory (red tassel) established in the first turn. Option A has the wrong color and tassel. Option C has the wrong color and charm type.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The model must track the specific attribute combination (azure color + red tassel) across three distinct visual contexts with varying lighting conditions. Relying only on the final image (Turn 5) makes it impossible to distinguish the 'correct' bag from the distractors without the context of what the user originally owned. Additionally, the assistant explicitly states uncertainty about matching them, preventing the model from relying on conversational cues rather than visual memory.

_meta.scenario: The conversation begins with a picture of a bright blue woven beach bag sitting on white sand next to a towel. Later, the user shares an indoor museum photo where the bag is slumped on a wooden bench near a display case, looking deflated and dimly lit under artificial gallery lighting.
